text: Welcome to This is My Architecture. Today I have here Sergey with me from PropertyFinder.
Hello Sergey. Hi Kamran. Can you tell me a bit about PropertyFinder? Sure. PropertyFinder is
online real estate portal with more than 300 employees representing more than 40 nationalities.
We have our headquarters here in UAE, Dubai, but we also have our team members in Egypt, Qatar,
Turkey, Bahrain and Saudi Arabia. So we have a very interesting architecture here. Can you tell
me about the use case what you're trying to solve? So when we decided to split our monolithic
application into the microservices, we came up to our mind that we can use the Kubernetes cluster
and the EKS. So you split the microservices. What kind of scale were you talking about in terms of
microservices? So as for now on the production environment we are running around 20 instances
containers and around 2,000 containers every day. So that's a very low scale. So let's look at a use
case that you have. Okay. So let's say a user wants to access our PropertyFinder.ae website. So first,
where he goes, he's hitting our application load balancer. Afterwards, our application load balancer is
actually routing him to our Kubernetes cluster where we have the website load running and our
ingress controller is actually going to this pod. I also noticed here you have EC2 spot instances in
all your availability zones. Can you tell me a bit more about that? Sure. So when we configured our
architecture, we figure out that we can run 100% on the spot instances, even in the production workloads.
That's very interesting. 100% spot instances. So how do you mitigate for spot failures?
So as you know, the spot instance can actually go down. So first, what we are doing, we have a proper
redundancy for all our services. So let's say the website is running in minimum three nodes in each of
the availability zones. So when we know that the one of the spot instances being marked for the termination,
we have two minutes period to do something. We are listening to the event breach for this particular
event. And then we're triggering the lambda function, which is actually scaling up our auto scaling group,
and draining the node, which is marked for the termination. So Sergey, what has been the benefit
of using this spot-based architecture? So the spot-based architecture is actually can help us to save up to 50%
on our production environment. Compared to on-demand instances, I'm assuming. Yes. That's great. So in terms of
evolution of your architecture, what's next? So the next step is to use managed node groups,
which can help us to get rid of these entire steps and give all the things to the AWS to manage our spot instances.
One last question for you. How do you monitor this environment? You are using spot instances,
Kubernetes clusters and pods. How do you monitor this environment? Yes. The monitoring is very important
because we are running on the spot instances. We use Datadoc, which is giving us all the things that we
need to monitor our Kubernetes cluster. Sergey, thank you for sharing your architecture with me today.
Thank you, Kamran. And thank you for joining This is My Architecture.
